steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The blank comes after “book packaging” and before the verb “specializes”. We need a noun to fill the blank and a connector to link it to the verb.

steps.title: Apply the relative clause rule
steps.content: The noun “company” fills the blank, and the verb “specializes” requires a relative pronoun to introduce it. The rule is that “that” introduces relative clauses without a preceding comma. “Company that specializes in...” correctly applies this rule, while options A and C lack the pronoun (leaving ungrammatical gaps) and option D incorrectly places a comma before “that”. That matches choice B — B is correct.
